parameters-en.Drive: Controls the amount of gain applied to the signal, which influences the level of distortion.
parameters-en.Bass: Controls the low frequency EQ of the tonestack.
parameters-en.Mid: Controls the midrange frequency EQ of the tonestack.
parameters-en.Treble: Controls the high frequency EQ of the tonestack.
parameters-en.Presence: Boosts upper mid and high frequencies in the preamp.
parameters-en.Level: Controls the overall level of the Preamp block. Unlike Master Volume, it has no effect on the block's tone.
parameters-en.Master: Controls the Master Volume of the amplifier.
parameters-en.Sag: Controls the amount of sag, or how much the power supply compresses or "droops" in response to striking the strings hard. Higher values provide more touch dynamics, sustain, and organic feel that's inherent in vintage tube amps; lower values offer a "tighter" responsiveness for a more modern feel.
parameters-en.80Hz: The amount of boost and cut is not equal for all bands. This is consistent with the real amp.
parameters-en.240Hz: The amount of boost and cut is not equal for all bands. This is consistent with the real amp.
parameters-en.750Hz: The amount of boost and cut is not equal for all bands. This is consistent with the real amp.
parameters-en.2200Hz: The amount of boost and cut is not equal for all bands. This is consistent with the real amp.
parameters-en.6600Hz: The amount of boost and cut is not equal for all bands. This is consistent with the real amp.
